channel.title: Last Day - NEW & UPDATE
channel.description: All NEW and UPDATE articles from the last day with enhanced metadata

item.title: NEW: Mini Shai-Hulud: Massive Supply Chain Attack Compromises SAP, PyTorch, and Intercom Packages
item.description:
Headline: Mini Shai-Hulud: Massive Supply Chain Attack Compromises SAP, PyTorch, and Intercom Packages

Summary:
A sophisticated supply chain attack campaign named "Mini Shai-Hulud," attributed to the threat actor group TeamPCP, has compromised popular open-source packages from SAP, PyTorch Lightning, and Intercom on both NPM and PyPI repositories. The attack, which began on April 29, 2026, involves injecting malicious preinstall scripts into widely used developer packages, collectively downloaded millions of times. The malware is designed to steal a vast array of credentials, including local secrets, GitHub tokens, and cloud service keys (AWS, Azure, GCP), exfiltrating them to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The campaign also features a worm-like propagation mechanism, using stolen GitHub tokens to inject malicious code into other repositories, significantly amplifying its reach and impacting an estimated 1,800 developers so far.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: Detailed analysis of the "Mini Shai-Hulud" supply chain attack that compromised SAP, PyTorch, and Intercom packages on NPM and PyPI, leading to credential theft from over 1,800 developers.

Article Slug: mini-shai-hulud-supply-chain-attack-hits-sap-lightning-intercom

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Copy.Fail: New Linux Kernel Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Allows Instant Root Access
item.description:
Headline: Copy.Fail: New Linux Kernel Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Allows Instant Root Access

Summary:
A critical high-severity vulnerability, dubbed "Copy.Fail" and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been discovered in the Linux kernel, affecting nearly all major distributions released since 2017. The flaw, rated with a CVSS score of 7.8, is a logic bug in the kernel's crypto API that allows an unprivileged local user to perform a controlled memory write. A publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit demonstrates how this can be used to modify a setuid binary like `/usr/bin/su` in memory, achieving full root access reliably and within seconds. The exploit is particularly dangerous in multi-tenant environments like Kubernetes and CI/CD systems, as it leaves no trace on disk, evading simple detection methods.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Discover the critical "Copy.Fail" Linux kernel vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431), which allows unprivileged local users to gain root access in seconds. Learn about affected systems and immediate patching needs.

Article Slug: copy-fail-linux-kernel-flaw-cve-2026-31431-root-access

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: cPanel Zero-Day Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-41940) Actively Exploited for Months Before Patch
item.description:
Headline: cPanel Zero-Day Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-41940) Actively Exploited for Months Before Patch

Summary:
A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-41940, in the widely used cPanel & WHM web hosting platform was actively exploited as a zero-day for at least two months before a patch was released. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to gain full administrative access to a vulnerable server. The vulnerability is a CRLF injection weakness that allows an attacker to manipulate session files and create an administrator-level session without credentials. Hosting providers observed exploitation as early as February 2026, long before the official advisory on April 28. With an estimated 1.5 million cPanel instances exposed online, administrators are urged to patch immediately.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A critical 9.8 CVSS zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel & WHM was exploited for months, allowing unauthenticated root access. Learn about the flaw and the urgent need to patch.

Article Slug: critical-cpanel-zero-day-cve-2026-41940-exploited-for-months

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Cybersecurity Pros Jailed for 4 Years for Aiding BlackCat Ransomware Gang
item.description:
Headline: Cybersecurity Pros Jailed for 4 Years for Aiding BlackCat Ransomware Gang

Summary:
In a significant case of insiders turning to crime, two American cybersecurity professionals, Ryan Goldberg and Kevin Martin, have each been sentenced to four years in prison. They pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges for acting as affiliates of the notorious BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware gang. Between April and December 2023, the defendants used their expert knowledge to conduct ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations, including a healthcare provider. They extorted victims, laundered payments, and in one case, leaked sensitive patient data. The case highlights the dangerous threat of malicious insiders and the abuse of specialized skills for criminal profit within the ransomware-as-a-service ecosystem.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Two US cybersecurity professionals have been sentenced to four years in prison for their roles as affiliates in the BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware operation, where they used their skills to attack victims.

Article Slug: cybersecurity-professionals-sentenced-for-blackcat-ransomware-attacks

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: QR Code Phishing Surges 146% in Q1 2026, Microsoft Warns
item.description:
Headline: QR Code Phishing Surges 146% in Q1 2026, Microsoft Warns

Summary:
According to Microsoft's Q1 2026 threat intelligence report, QR code-based phishing (quishing) has become the fastest-growing email attack vector, with volumes jumping 146% between January and March. Attackers are increasingly hiding malicious QR codes within PDF attachments to bypass email security scanners. These campaigns, often using CAPTCHA-gated landing pages to further evade detection, aim to steal user credentials. In Q1, Microsoft detected 8.3 billion email phishing threats in total, with one major campaign in March targeting 179,000 organizations across 43 countries, highlighting the scale and sophistication of modern Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) operations like Tycoon2FA.

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: Microsoft's Q1 2026 threat report shows QR code phishing (quishing) is the fastest-growing email threat, up 146%. Attackers use PDFs and CAPTCHAs to steal credentials.

Article Slug: microsoft-report-qr-code-phishing-attacks-skyrocket-146-percent

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ransomware Civil War: KryBit RaaS Hacks and Leaks Rival Gang 0APT
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Civil War: KryBit RaaS Hacks and Leaks Rival Gang 0APT

Summary:
In an unusual turn of events within the cybercrime underworld, the new KryBit ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group has hacked and publicly leaked the entire operational infrastructure of a rival gang, 0APT. The leak appears to be retaliation for 0APT previously leaking some of KryBit's data. The exposed data revealed that 0APT had been fabricating victim claims and was running its leak site from a shockingly amateur setup: an Android device. While 0APT is left exposed, KryBit continues its own attacks, employing double extortion tactics against at least 20 victims across multiple sectors and countries, including the US, Germany, and Japan.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: In a rare public feud, the KryBit ransomware group has hacked and dismantled a rival operation, 0APT, leaking data that exposes 0APT's amateur setup and fabricated claims.

Article Slug: ransomware-civil-war-krybit-raas-leaks-rival-gang-0apt

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan
item.description:
Headline: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan

Summary:
The UK government's latest Cyber Security Breaches Survey for 2025/2026 reveals a landscape of persistent risk, with 43% of businesses (an estimated 612,000) experiencing a breach in the past year. Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, implicated in 85% of successful breaches. Despite the high frequency of attacks, the report exposes critical gaps in organizational readiness: only 25% of businesses have a formal incident response plan. The survey also highlights major weaknesses in supply chain risk management and a lack of security practices for adopting AI, prompting criticism that current UK policy is insufficient to address national cyber risk.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: The UK Government's 2026 Cyber Security Breaches Survey shows 43% of businesses were breached, but critical gaps remain in incident response, supply chain risk, and AI security.

Article Slug: uk-cyber-security-breaches-survey-2026-reveals-critical-gaps

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: ADT Security Hit by Data Breach; ShinyHunters Claims 5.5 Million Records Stolen
item.description:
Headline: ADT Security Hit by Data Breach; ShinyHunters Claims 5.5 Million Records Stolen

Summary:
Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach that exposed the personal information of 5.5 million unique individuals. The incident was initiated by a voice phishing (vishing) attack on an employee, which allowed the extortion group ShinyHunters to gain access to the employee's Okta single sign-on account. The attackers then pivoted to ADT's Salesforce instance to exfiltrate customer data. While ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen 10 million records, analysis by Have I Been Pwned confirmed 5.5 million unique email addresses. ADT states the compromised data was 'limited' to names, addresses, and phone numbers, and that no financial or security system data was accessed.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Home security provider ADT confirms a major data breach affecting 5.5 million individuals after a vishing attack allowed ShinyHunters to compromise an Okta SSO account and access Salesforce data.

Article Slug: adt-security-data-breach-shinyhunters-claims-10-million-records

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: AI-Enabled Cybercrime Fuels 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims, Fortinet Reports
item.description:
Headline: AI-Enabled Cybercrime Fuels 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims, Fortinet Reports

Summary:
The 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report from Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs reveals a dramatic industrialization of cybercrime, heavily augmented by artificial intelligence. The report highlights a staggering 389% year-over-year increase in confirmed ransomware victims, a surge attributed to the proliferation of AI-powered crime-as-a-service tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT. Furthermore, AI is enabling attackers to move with unprecedented speed, shrinking the average time-to-exploit for critical vulnerabilities to just 24-48 hours. This acceleration leaves defenders with a dangerously small window to respond and underscores a fundamental shift in the threat landscape towards faster, more sophisticated, and scalable attacks.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Ransomware, Malware

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Fortinet's 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report finds AI-powered tools are driving a 389% increase in ransomware victims and cutting vulnerability exploit times to under 48 hours.

Article Slug: fortinet-report-ai-cybercrime-drives-389-percent-ransomware-surge

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: MOVEit Automation Hit with Critical 9.8 CVSS Auth Bypass Flaw (CVE-2026-4670)
item.description:
Headline: MOVEit Automation Hit with Critical 9.8 CVSS Auth Bypass Flaw (CVE-2026-4670)

Summary:
Progress Software has issued an urgent security alert for a critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-4670, in its MOVEit Automation software. The flaw, which has been assigned a CVSS score of 9.8, could allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain unauthorized access and potentially administrative control over the system. Given the history of mass exploitation targeting the MOVEit product suite, this vulnerability poses a significant risk. Progress has released patched versions and strongly urges all customers to upgrade immediately, noting that the update requires a full installer and will cause a system outage.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: Progress Software discloses CVE-2026-4670, a critical 9.8 CVSS authentication bypass vulnerability in MOVEit Automation. All users are urged to patch immediately to prevent unauthorized access.

Article Slug: critical-authentication-bypass-vulnerability-in-moveit-automation-cve-2026-4670

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Portkey, an AI Gateway Startup, to Secure Autonomous AI Agents
item.description:
Headline: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Portkey, an AI Gateway Startup, to Secure Autonomous AI Agents

Summary:
Palo Alto Networks has announced its planned acquisition of Portkey, a startup specializing in AI Gateway technology. The move is a strategic effort to address the emerging security challenges posed by the rise of autonomous AI agents in the enterprise. Portkey provides a centralized control plane to manage, monitor, and secure AI agent traffic. Its technology will be integrated into Palo Alto Networks' Prisma AIRS platform, creating a 'central nervous system' to inspect AI transactions, enforce governance, and apply least-privilege controls. This acquisition reflects a growing industry focus on securing the new, unmanaged attack surface created by highly privileged AI agents.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: Palo Alto Networks announces its intent to acquire Portkey, a startup specializing in AI Gateway technology, to secure the growing attack surface of autonomous AI agents within its Prisma AIRS platform.

Article Slug: palo-alto-networks-to-acquire-portkey-to-secure-autonomous-ai-agents

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CISA Mandates Patch for Actively Exploited Windows NTLM Hash Leak Flaw
item.description:
Headline: CISA Mandates Patch for Actively Exploited Windows NTLM Hash Leak Flaw

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a Windows information disclosure vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog due to evidence of active exploitation. The zero-click flaw allows an attacker to steal NTLM hashes (hashed user passwords) from a target machine, which can then be used in pass-the-hash attacks for lateral movement. The vulnerability exists due to an incomplete patch for a previous flaw. CISA has issued a directive requiring all Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the necessary patches by May 12, 2026, highlighting the significant risk it poses.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Policy and Compliance

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: CISA adds Windows NTLM hash leak vulnerability CVE-2026-32202 to its KEV catalog, mandating federal agencies to patch the actively exploited, zero-click flaw by May 12, 2026.

Article Slug: cisa-orders-patch-for-exploited-windows-ntlm-hash-leak-flaw-cve-2026-32202

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data
item.description:
Headline: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data

Summary:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has discovered at least 18 malicious browser extensions masquerading as Generative AI productivity tools. These extensions, some downloaded by thousands, are not enhancing productivity but are instead functioning as Remote Access Trojans (RATs), infostealers, and search hijackers. The attackers leverage the broad permissions users grant to these 'AI assistants' to intercept sensitive data, including credentials, proprietary code shared in AI prompts, and private communications. The research provides technical case studies, including an extension that establishes a persistent command-and-control channel to execute arbitrary code within a user's authenticated sessions, highlighting a severe risk to both individuals and enterprises.

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Article Type: Report

Keywords: AI browser extension, malicious extension, GenAI security, Remote Access Trojan, infostealer malware, Unit 42 research, browser security, Chrome malware, data exfiltration, cybersecurity threat

Reading Time: 13 minutes

Meta Description: Unit 42's investigation reveals that popular AI-powered browser extensions are being used as a vector for malware, including RATs and infostealers, to steal sensitive user data, passwords, and proprietary information from GenAI prompts.

Article Slug: malicious-ai-browser-extensions-reading-emails-and-stealing-data

Published Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang

Summary:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the notorious BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware gang. Martino abused his position at a crypto brokerage firm, using his insider knowledge of his clients' negotiating strategies and insurance limits to help BlackCat maximize their extortion demands. He also conspired to deploy ransomware against victims, leading to multi-million dollar ransom payments. Law enforcement has seized $10 million in illicit proceeds.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Former ransomware negotiator Angelo Martino has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware gang, using his insider access to help them extort his own clients.

Article Slug: former-ransomware-negotiator-pleads-guilty-to-aiding-blackcat-gang

Published Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: TeamPCP Weaponizes npm with Malicious Bitwarden CLI in Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Headline: TeamPCP Weaponizes npm with Malicious Bitwarden CLI in Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack

Summary:
A sophisticated supply chain attack targeting the npm ecosystem has been uncovered by Unit 42, attributed to the threat actor group TeamPCP. The attackers published a malicious package, `@bitwarden/cli` version `2026.4.0`, which perfectly impersonates the legitimate Bitwarden command-line tool. Upon installation, the package executes a multi-stage, heavily obfuscated payload designed to harvest credentials from developer workstations, cloud environments (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), and CI/CD pipelines. The malware exhibits worm-like capabilities, inspired by the 2025 "Shai-Hulud" worm, by automatically backdooring and republishing other npm packages to which the compromised developer has access, ensuring its rapid propagation. The campaign, which also targeted other distribution channels like a VS Code extension, utilized a centralized C2 infrastructure at `audit.checkmarx[.]cx`, indicating a coordinated effort to compromise developer tooling.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Reading Time: 12 minutes

Meta Description: Unit 42 details a sophisticated npm supply chain attack by TeamPCP using a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to harvest credentials and propagate like a worm. Learn the TTPs and how to mitigate this threat.

Article Slug: npm-threat-landscape-teampcp-supply-chain-attack-shai-hulud

Published Date: Sat, 25 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 25 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
item.description:
Headline: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data

Summary:
A rare public conflict has erupted between two rival ransomware groups, 0APT and KryBit. The feud, which played out between March 28 and April 12, 2026, involved both groups hacking each other and leaking sensitive operational data. 0APT initiated the spat by leaking KryBit's admin panel. KryBit retaliated by hacking 0APT, defacing their leak site, and dumping their entire operational dataset. The leak revealed that 0APT had fabricated over 190 victim listings and was running its infrastructure on an Android phone. Security experts believe the mutual doxing will likely force both amateurish groups to rebuild and rebrand.

Severity: Low

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: A public feud between ransomware groups 0APT and KryBit resulted in both hacking each other, leading to leaked operational data and the exposure of 0APT as a fraudulent, amateur operation.

Article Slug: ransomware-turf-war-0apt-and-krybit-groups-hack-each-other

Published Date: Wed, 29 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 29 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Actively Exploited Windows Zero-Day (CVE-2026-32202) Steals NTLM Hashes Without User Clicks
item.description:
Headline: Actively Exploited Windows Zero-Day (CVE-2026-32202) Steals NTLM Hashes Without User Clicks

Summary:
Microsoft and CISA are warning of active, zero-click exploitation of a Windows Shell spoofing vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202. The flaw, which stems from an incomplete patch for a bug previously used by APT28, allows attackers to steal Net-NTLMv2 hashes simply by having a user open a folder containing a malicious file. This enables NTLM relay and pass-the-hash attacks. CISA has added the vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, mandating that U.S. federal agencies apply the patch immediately.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Threat Actor

Meta Description: Active exploitation of Windows Shell vulnerability CVE-2026-32202 allows for zero-click NTLM hash theft. CISA has added the flaw to its KEV catalog, mandating immediate patching.

Article Slug: microsoft-warns-actively-exploited-windows-zero-day-cve-2026-32202-credential-theft

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Fortinet Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Fuel 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims
item.description:
Headline: Fortinet Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Fuel 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims

Summary:
Fortinet's 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report reveals a staggering 389% year-over-year increase in confirmed ransomware victims in 2025, attributing the surge in part to AI-enabled cybercrime. The report highlights how tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT are making attacks more sophisticated and scalable. A key finding is the dramatic reduction in the time-to-exploit for critical vulnerabilities, which has shrunk to between 24 and 48 hours, demanding a more agile and industrialized defense.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Ransomware, Malware

Meta Description: Fortinet's 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report finds AI-enabled cybercrime is driving a 389% increase in ransomware victims and shrinking attack timelines to under 48 hours.

Article Slug: fortinet-report-ai-enabled-attacks-fuel-389-percent-surge-in-ransomware

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 01 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros
item.description:
Headline: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros

Summary:
A critical logic flaw in the Linux kernel, dubbed 'Copy Fail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been disclosed, affecting nearly all major distributions since 2017. Discovered by Theori, the vulnerability allows any unprivileged local user to gain full root privileges with a simple, highly reliable exploit that does not depend on race conditions. This poses a severe risk to multi-tenant environments like cloud hosting, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. A public proof-of-concept exists, and major Linux vendors are rushing to release patches.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Patch Management

Meta Description: A critical local privilege escalation vulnerability, CVE-2026-31431 ('Copy Fail'), in the Linux kernel allows reliable root access on major distributions. Learn about the impact, affected systems, and mitigation.

Article Slug: critical-copy-fail-linux-kernel-flaw-cve-2026-31431-root-access

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Critical GitHub RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-3854) Allowed Full Server Compromise via Single 'git push'
item.description:
Headline: Critical GitHub RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-3854) Allowed Full Server Compromise via Single 'git push'

Summary:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in GitHub, tracked as CVE-2026-3854, has been disclosed by security firm Wiz. The flaw allowed any authenticated user with push access to a repository to execute arbitrary commands on GitHub's backend servers using a specially crafted `git push` command. The bug affected both GitHub.com and self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) instances, potentially leading to the compromise of millions of repositories or full server takeovers. Patches are available, but a high percentage of internet-facing GHES instances remain vulnerable.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack, Cloud Security

Meta Description: Details on the critical RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-3854 in GitHub and GitHub Enterprise Server, which allowed command injection and potential repository compromise via 'git push'.

Article Slug: critical-github-rce-flaw-cve-2026-3854-exposed-millions-of-repositories

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data
item.description:
Headline: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data

Summary:
Medical device manufacturer Medtronic has confirmed it sustained a cybersecurity incident following claims by the ShinyHunters threat group. The group alleged it stole over 9 million records and terabytes of corporate data, issuing a 'pay or leak' ultimatum. Medtronic stated the breach was contained to its corporate IT systems and, due to network segmentation, had no impact on medical products, patient safety, or manufacturing operations. An investigation into the scope of the data access is ongoing.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Meta Description: Medical technology giant Medtronic confirms a corporate data breach after the ShinyHunters group claimed to steal 9 million records. Patient safety and medical devices were not affected.

Article Slug: medtronic-confirms-breach-after-shinyhunters-claims-theft-of-9-million-records

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Critical Flaw in VECT 2.0 Ransomware Turns It Into a Destructive Wiper, Permanently Destroying Large Files
item.description:
Headline: Critical Flaw in VECT 2.0 Ransomware Turns It Into a Destructive Wiper, Permanently Destroying Large Files

Summary:
Check Point Research has discovered a critical implementation flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes it to permanently destroy any file larger than 128KB, effectively turning it into a data wiper. The bug, found in all variants targeting Windows, Linux, and ESXi, relates to improper handling of cryptographic nonces. This means that even if a victim pays the ransom, recovery of most valuable enterprise data is impossible, as the keys required for decryption are discarded during the encryption process.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Intelligence

Meta Description: A critical flaw in VECT 2.0 ransomware causes it to act as a data wiper, permanently destroying files over 128KB on Windows, Linux, and ESXi systems. Recovery is impossible even if the ransom is paid.

Article Slug: vect-2-0-ransomware-flaw-turns-malware-into-destructive-wiper

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 15-Year-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Stealthy Root Access via Crafted Certificate
item.description:
Headline: 15-Year-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Stealthy Root Access via Crafted Certificate

Summary:
A high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-35414 or 'SplitSSHell', has been discovered in OpenSSH versions released over the past 15 years. With a CVSS score of 8.1, the flaw allows an authenticated attacker with a valid certificate to use a specially crafted principal name to bypass access controls and gain full root shell access. A major concern is that the attack is stealthy, leaving no trace in standard system logs, which makes detection extremely difficult. All organizations are urged to update to OpenSSH 10.3 or newer.

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Threat Intelligence

Meta Description: A 15-year-old vulnerability in OpenSSH, CVE-2026-35414 ('SplitSSHell'), allows authenticated attackers to gain stealthy root access via crafted certificates. Update to OpenSSH 10.3 is critical.

Article Slug: 15-year-old-openssh-flaw-cve-2026-35414-allows-root-access

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments
item.description:
Headline: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), in partnership with the Department of War, DOE, FBI, and DOS, has published a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles within Operational Technology (OT) environments. The guidance provides a roadmap for securing critical infrastructure, addressing the unique challenges of OT such as legacy systems, uptime requirements, and the paramount need for physical process safety, especially in the face of threats like Volt Typhoon.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems, Regulatory

Meta Description: CISA and U.S. government partners have released a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles in Operational Technology (OT) environments to protect critical infrastructure.

Article Slug: us-government-releases-joint-guidance-for-zero-trust-in-ot

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
item.description:
Headline: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave

Summary:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortion, moving away from pure encryption. It also notes a concerning trend of state-sponsored actors using cybercriminal groups as proxies, blurring the lines between espionage and financially motivated crime.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Ransomware

Meta Description: Europol's 2026 IOCTA report details the rise of industrialized cybercrime, fueled by AI, encryption, and a strategic shift in ransomware tactics toward data extortion and hybrid threats.

Article Slug: europol-iocta-report-ai-encryption-fuel-industrialized-cybercrime

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion
item.description:
Headline: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion

Summary:
The ShinyHunters cybercrime group has publicly leaked a database containing 1.4 million records from the online learning platform Udemy after a 'pay or leak' extortion attempt failed. The compromised data is extensive, including user and instructor PII, contact information, and sensitive financial payout details like PayPal accounts and bank information. The breach places affected individuals at a high risk of phishing, fraud, and identity theft. Users are advised to reset passwords and enable 2FA.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Phishing

Meta Description: ShinyHunters has leaked 1.4 million user and instructor records from the online learning platform Udemy, exposing PII and highly sensitive financial payout information.

Article Slug: shinyhunters-leaks-1-4m-udemy-user-records-after-failed-extortion

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: HITRUST Analysis Confirms Significant Rise in AI-Enabled Cyberattacks in Q1 2026
item.description:
Headline: HITRUST Analysis Confirms Significant Rise in AI-Enabled Cyberattacks in Q1 2026

Summary:
HITRUST's Q1 2026 Cyber Threat Adaptive (CTA) analysis confirms a significant and continued increase in AI-enabled cyberattacks. The report details how adversaries are leveraging AI to automate deception (e.g., deepfakes, advanced phishing), discover complex vulnerabilities, and exploit AI-enabled systems directly. This trend is compressing the time between vulnerability discovery and exploitation, highlighting the need for dynamic, threat-adaptive security frameworks like the HITRUST CSF to keep pace with the evolving threat landscape.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Phishing

Meta Description: HITRUST's Q1 2026 Cyber Threat Adaptive analysis highlights a significant rise in AI-enabled cyberattacks, including AI-enhanced phishing, deepfakes, and exploitation of AI tools.

Article Slug: hitrust-report-shows-ai-enabled-attacks-are-on-the-rise

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service
item.description:
Headline: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service

Summary:
Security researchers from Securonix have discovered a sophisticated, multi-stage Python backdoor named DEEP#DOOR. The malware operates as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan (RAT), beginning with an obfuscated batch script that embeds the Python payload. It disables Windows security controls, establishes persistence through multiple methods (Startup folder, Run keys, Scheduled Tasks, WMI), and uses the legitimate 'bore.pub' tunneling service for stealthy command-and-control and data exfiltration, focusing on browser and cloud credentials.

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Meta Description: Securonix researchers detail DEEP#DOOR, a new stealthy Python backdoor that functions as a RAT to steal credentials from Windows systems by abusing a legitimate tunneling service for C2.

Article Slug: stealthy-python-backdoor-deepdoor-steals-credentials-via-tunneling

Published Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Patching Windows Collapse as Time-to-Exploit for Vulnerabilities Shrinks Dramatically
item.description:
Headline: Patching Windows Collapse as Time-to-Exploit for Vulnerabilities Shrinks Dramatically

Summary:
The window of opportunity for IT security teams to apply patches is collapsing, according to a new report from Rapid7. The median time from a vulnerability's disclosure to its active exploitation has been cut nearly in half, dropping from 8.5 to just 5.0 days for inclusion in CISA's KEV catalog. This acceleration is driven by the industrialization of cybercrime and the use of AI to rapidly develop exploits. The trend signifies a major shift in the threat landscape, where 'n-day' vulnerabilities (known flaws with patches available) now pose a greater practical threat than zero-days, putting immense pressure on defenders to automate and accelerate their patching cycles.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Patch Management, Security Operations

Article Type: Analysis

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A new Rapid7 report shows the time-to-exploit for vulnerabilities has been cut in half, driven by AI and cybercrime industrialization, putting immense pressure on security teams to accelerate patching.

Article Slug: vulnerability-patching-windows-collapse-as-exploitation-times-accelerate

Published Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Unit 42: Frontier AI Models Can Autonomously Find Zero-Days, Posing Major Threat to Software Security
item.description:
Headline: Unit 42: Frontier AI Models Can Autonomously Find Zero-Days, Posing Major Threat to Software Security

Summary:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has conducted hands-on research with frontier AI models, revealing their alarming capability to act as autonomous security researchers. These models can independently identify zero-day vulnerabilities and complex exploit chains, posing a significant and immediate risk to the software ecosystem, especially open-source software (OSS). The research indicates that these AIs dramatically lower the barrier for unskilled attackers and accelerate the vulnerability-to-exploitation timeline from N-days to N-hours. Unit 42 predicts a surge in large-scale, AI-driven supply chain attacks and urges defenders to adopt an aggressive, prevention-first security posture to counter the unprecedented speed and scale of these emerging threats.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Supply Chain Attack, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Reading Time: 8 minutes

Meta Description: Unit 42 research reveals frontier AI models can autonomously find and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities, significantly increasing risks for open-source software and accelerating the entire attack lifecycle.

Article Slug: fracturing-software-security-with-frontier-ai-models

Published Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 2

Update History: 2 updates recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Decade-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Full Root Access, Exploits Hard to Detect
item.description:
Headline: Decade-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Full Root Access, Exploits Hard to Detect

Summary:
A critical remote code execution vulnerability, CVE-2026-35414, has been discovered in OpenSSH, affecting versions released over the last 15 years. The flaw, which carries a CVSS score of 8.1, resides in the parsing of SSH certificate principal names. An attacker with a valid certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority (CA) can craft a principal name with a comma (e.g., 'deploy,root') to trick the server into granting root-level access. A major concern is that successful exploitation is logged as a legitimate authentication, making detection through log analysis nearly impossible. The vulnerability affects OpenSSH versions prior to 10.3, and administrators are urged to update to the patched version 10.3p1 immediately to prevent full system compromise.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Meta Description: A critical 15-year-old vulnerability in OpenSSH, CVE-2026-35414 (CVSS 8.1), allows remote attackers to gain full root access. Learn how the flaw works and why you must patch to version 10.3p1 immediately.

Article Slug: critical-15-year-old-openssh-flaw-cve-2026-35414-allows-root-access

Published Date: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Windows Flaw to KEV Catalog After Botched Patch for APT28 Zero-Day
item.description:
Headline: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Windows Flaw to KEV Catalog After Botched Patch for APT28 Zero-Day

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a new Microsoft Windows vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, mandating that federal agencies patch it. The vulnerability, which is being actively exploited, is an authentication coercion flaw resulting from an incomplete patch for a previous zero-day, CVE-2026-21510. The original flaw was exploited by the Russian APT group APT28 (Fancy Bear) in attacks against Ukraine and the EU. The new zero-click vulnerability can be used to steal credentials via specially crafted LNK files and was fixed in Microsoft's April 2026 security updates.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Threat Actor

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: CISA has added CVE-2026-32202, an actively exploited Windows vulnerability resulting from an incomplete patch for an APT28 zero-day, to its KEV catalog. Patching is urgent.

Article Slug: cisa-adds-actively-exploited-windows-flaw-cve-2026-32202-to-kev-catalog

Published Date: Wed, 29 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 29 Apr 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →
